title: Add support for criu's tcp-close flag
body:
### Feature request description

Currently, podman is able to checkpoint a container then restore it.  If there are no active TCP connections in the container, then podman is able to restore multiple copies of the container with different --name values.  If there are active TCP connections, then podman must checkpoint the container with the --tcp-established flag.  When restoring that container, podman currently has to use the --tcp-established flag to tell crun then criu to try to re-establish the TCP connections.  The --tcp-established flag is not compatible with the --name flag, though.  This means you cannot restore multiple copies of the container.  The criu restore command supports an alternative to --tcp-established called --tcp-close.  If podman and crun supported the --tcp-close flag, then podman would be able to restore multiple copies of the container.  The impact is that the container would not try to re-established those connections.  Whatever is running inside of the container would need to detect that their TCP connections were terminated and would need to re-establish the connections themselves.  I made local modifies to crun and podman to add support for the --tcp-close flag, and I was able to do a proof of concept test.  I have submitted an issue to crun to add support: [crun #1833](https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/1833)

### Suggest potential solution

I would like podman to add support for the --tcp-close flag.  This flag would be passed to crun which would then be passed to criu (which already supports the flag).

### Have you considered any alternatives?

I haven't figured out any other way for podman to restore multiple copies of a checkpoint that has established TCP connections.

### Additional context

In my proof of concept, I was able to run the following commands:

> \# Create 3 pods
> podman pod create -p myip:2001:8080 p1
> podman pod create -p myip:2002:8080 p2
> podman pod create -p myip:2003:8080 p3
> \# Start the petstore example in pod p1
> podman run -d -e OPENAPI_BASE_PATH=/v3 --name app1 --pod p1 openapitools/openapi-petstore
> \# Start a locust test to send traffic before continuing to the next steps
> \# This ensures that there are active TCP connections going into the petstore app
> \# Checkpoint the first container
> podman container checkpoint app1 --tcp-established -e /tmp/checkpoint.tar.zstd
> \# Restore the container into another pod.  This would fail without --tcp-close
> podman container restore --pod p2 --name app2 --tcp-close -i /tmp/checkpoint.tar.zstd
> \# Restore the container into another pod.  This would also fail without --tcp-close
> podman container restore --pod p3 --name app3 --tcp-close -i /tmp/checkpoint.tar.zstd

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
